commit.message:
feat: import export assistant configuration (#1284)

* feat(backend,frontend): add import/export functionality for assistant configurations

Add backend endpoints and frontend support for exporting and importing assistant configurations as JSON files with masked sensitive data.

Backend changes:
- Add ExportConfiguration query handler to export configurations with masked passwords
- Add ImportConfiguration command handler to validate and import configurations
- Add ExportedConfigurationDto and ImportConfigurationDto for API
- Add GET /configurations/:id/export and POST /configurations/import endpoints
- Register new handlers in ExtensionModule

Frontend changes:
- Regenerate API client with new export/import endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(frontend): add import/export UI for assistant configurations

Add frontend UI components for importing and exporting assistant configurations with user-friendly error handling.

Frontend changes:
- Add export menu item to Configuration dropdown with JSON download
- Add import button to ConfigurationPage header with file upload
- Add i18n translations for EN/DE (export, import, success/error messages)
- Sanitize filenames on export (alphanumerics, hyphens, underscores, spaces)
- Validate JSON structure on import with error toasts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(backend): add unit tests for export/import configuration use cases

Add comprehensive unit tests for export and import functionality:

Export tests:
- Test exporting configuration with masked password values
- Test exporting configuration without extensions
- Test exporting configuration with multiple extensions
- Test NotFoundException when configuration doesn't exist

Import tests:
- Test BadRequestException when extensions are unavailable
- Test successful import with valid data
- Test importing disabled configurations
- Test unmasking password values during import
- Test correct externalId creation
- Test handling configurable arguments

All 11 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(backend): add logging to import configuration handler

Add comprehensive logging for import configuration operations:
- Log error when extensions are not available in the system
- Log error when extension configuration validation fails
- Log error when configuration reload fails after save
- Log success with configuration name, ID, and extension count

Logging helps with debugging and auditing import operations.

importing a configuration. Throws BadRequestException if none of the
specified groups exist, and logs a warning if some groups are missing

commit.message:
Fix [AUDIT-011] Add pool trading disabled flag to LedgerContext and exchange path

stellar-core checks isPoolTradingDisabled() in convertWithOffersAndPools
(OfferExchange.cpp:1396) before attempting pool exchange. Henyey was missing
this check — when the DISABLE_LIQUIDITY_POOL_TRADING_FLAG was set, path
payments would still route through pools.

Changes:
- Add ledger_flags field to LedgerContext with is_pool_trading_disabled() helper
- Check the flag in convert_with_offers_and_pools before pool evaluation
- Propagate ledger_flags from TransactionExecutor into LedgerContext in apply path
- Propagate ledger_flags in herder's ValidationContext::to_ledger_context()

Also closes #1204 (ManageOffer pool crossing) which was a false positive —
stellar-core skips pool exchange for RoundingType::NORMAL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: crypto-side playtest — BUG-041–048 (NFT minting, loot tiers, CAPS economy, scrap lock) (#217)

* Initial plan

* fix: crypto-side playtest bugs BUG-041 through BUG-048

BUG-041 (High) - mint-item.js: Replace non-atomic GET+SET daily-limit
check with atomic redis.incr() + expire() + decr-on-exceed to eliminate
TOCTOU race where concurrent requests could bypass the mint daily cap.

BUG-042 (High) - Tiered loot CAPS economy non-functional:
- loot-voucher.js: Calculate CAPS reward from POI rarity (RARITY_CAPS
  map with env-configurable values per tier) and sign it into the voucher
  as voucher.caps so the redemption amount is server-authenticated.
- gps.js: Include voucher.caps in serializeVoucherMessage (backward-
  compatible: old vouchers without caps field still verify correctly).
- redeem-voucher.js: Use voucher.caps directly (covered by NaCl
  signature) instead of broken LOOT_ID_TO_CAPS comma-delimited parser.

BUG-043 (Medium) - loot-voucher.js: Expand randomInt lootId range from
1,000,000 to 2^48 to dramatically reduce birthday-paradox PDA seed
collision probability for on-chain ClaimLoot.

BUG-044 (Medium) - redeem-voucher.js: Pre-call redis.key() in
VOUCHER_USED_KEY so voucher replay-protection keys are stored at
afw:afw:voucher:used:* (double-prefix, consistent with all other
app keys) instead of afw:voucher:used:* (orphaned single-prefix).

BUG-046 (Low) - caps.js: Add capsBalanceLimiter (30 req/min) to
GET /:wallet to prevent bulk wallet enumeration without authentication.

BUG-047 (Low) - player-state.js + dungeon.js: Add MAX_CAPS = 999_999_999
ceiling to awardCaps() and dungeon loot assignment to match backend limit
and prevent Pip-Boy display overflow.

BUG-048 (Low) - scrap-nft.js: Use per-NFT lock key (wallet+nftMint)
instead of per-wallet lock so concurrent scraps of different NFTs are
no longer unnecessarily serialized with 15-second waits.

Add 11 regression tests (BUG-041..048) — total test count: 94 → 105.

commit.message:
fix: resolve symlinks in detect_shell_config for sed -i compat

macOS sed -i fails silently on symlinked files (e.g. ~/.zshrc -> dotfiles).
This caused add/remove_env_from_shell_config to never actually delete
old entries, resulting in duplicate env vars accumulating in .zshrc.

Fix: resolve symlinks via readlink -f in detect_shell_config so sed
operates on the real file.

Also fixed: ~/.claude/settings.json had reversed key-value pairs in
the env section due to a prior bug.

commit.message:
Fix [AUDIT-004] store_quorum_set now unblocks fetching envelopes

store_quorum_set only updated ScpDriver and the quorum tracker, leaving
FetchingEnvelopes unaware of newly stored quorum sets. Envelopes blocked
waiting for a quorum set would never get unblocked through the app
consensus handler path (which calls store_quorum_set + clear_quorum_set_request
but not recv_quorum_set).

Mirror the quorum set receipt into FetchingEnvelopes at the end of
store_quorum_set so blocked envelopes get unblocked.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix double-apply of fee/seq/signer on cluster op failure in pre-parallel path

When pre_parallel_apply commits fee/seq/signer mutations to the main delta,
and a cluster's apply_body later fails, rollback_failed_tx was calling
restore_delta_entries which re-recorded those same mutations on the cluster
delta. Merging the cluster delta back into the main delta would then
double-apply them.

Fix: clear the rollback DeltaEntries in execute_with_pre_apply_result before
calling apply_body. On operation failure, restore_delta_entries sees empty
lists and becomes a no-op — matching stellar-core's parallelApply which
simply sets txFAILED without touching fee/seq/signer state (already
committed by preParallelApply on the shared ledger txn).

Meta generation (tx_changes_before, fee_changes) is unaffected — those are
separate fields in PreApplyResult.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
